items.title: Stevens hard voor Nederlandse clubs: 'PSV had tussenronde moeten halen'
items.summary: Huub Stevens loopt al een leven lang mee in de voetballerij. De 72-jarige oud-prof en succestrainer laat in een groot interview met Voetbal International onder meer zijn licht schijnen op de internationale prestaties van de Nederlandse clubs.

items.title: De Jong windt zich op over VAR-moment: 'Dit is een schandaal'
items.summary: Frenkie de Jong was na afloop niet te spreken over het optreden van de VAR tijdens de bekerwedstrijd tussen Atlético Madrid en Barcelona . Net na rust hoopten de Catalanen de comeback in te zetten, maar de goal van Pau Cubarsí werd afgekeurd. Daar was na afloop veel over te doen.

items.title: Ireland captain Stirling out of T20 World Cup
items.summary: Ireland captain Paul Stirling has been ruled out of the rest of the T20 World Cup because of a knee injury.

items.title: Arteta erkent dat Arsenal zich niet goed wapende tegen specialiteit Brentford
items.summary: Arsenal leed donderdagavond duur puntenverlies in de titelstrijd tegen Brentford ( 1-1 ), dat wederom scoorde uit zijn specialiteit: een verre inworp. Het was al de tiende keer dit seizoen dat The Bees op die manier scoorden, verreweg het meeste van alle Premier League-teams. Toch ging zijn ploeg daar niet goed mee om, erkende manager Mikel Arteta na afloop.

items.title: 'I had to put everyone on edge' - how managers make an impact
items.summary: In his BBC Sport column, ex-Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains the key things a manager can do before, during and after matches to get the results they want.

items.title: Is Price back to his best? 'There's no way I can't pick up a major'
items.summary: Gerwyn Price has warned his rivals he is back playing some of the best darts of his career and says a major title is just around the corner after winning Night Two of the Premier League in Antwerp.

items.title: Juby Ball and The Four Sixes clash at Chepstow
items.summary: We have a busy day’s racing on Friday with action from Chepstow, Wolverhampton and Meydan, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: F1 Testing LIVE! Russell fastest as Hamilton returns for final day
Golden Boy Quiz: Feyenoorder Sterling en zijn medewinnaars
items.summary: Met Raheem Sterling heeft Feyenoord een Golden Boy aangetrokken . In 2014 won de kersverse Feyenoorder de prestigieuze award, uitgereikt door Tuttosport .

items.title: Spaanse pers noemt Casadó naast De Jong 'Flicks ongekende fout'
items.summary: De Spaanse media wezen na afloop van de bekerafstraffing van Barcelona door Atlético Madrid naar trainer Hansi Flick. De keuze om Frenkie de Jong niet als enige controleur neer te zetten, pakte verkeerd uit. Zijn partner Marc Casadó speelde een dramatische wedstrijd.

items.title: Are football fans booing their team more?
items.summary: Booing at football is an accepted part of the game. It's been around since time began, but are fans increasingly booing their own team?

items.title: Cameron: With three-minute rounds I'd have knocked out Taylor!
items.summary: Chantelle Cameron is set to fight over three-minute rounds, the distance in men's bouts, when she boxes to become a world champion in a second weight class.

items.title: Rice tells Arsenal to ignore noise after PL lead cut
items.summary: Declan Rice has urged Arsenal to "block out the outside noise" but insisted his teammates remain calm after missing the opportunity to move six points clear.

items.title: Barcelona taught 'lesson' by Atlético in Copa rout
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his side were taught a lesson by Atlético Madrid as they suffered a 4-0 loss at the Metropolitano on Thursday in the first leg of the Copa del Rey semifinal between the two teams.

items.title: FA: Tuchel's new deal not related to Man Utd job
items.summary: Football Association chief executive Mark Bullingham said there are "performance conditions" connected to Thomas Tuchel staying on as England head coach beyond the World Cup.

items.title: 'Incredible' Álvarez leads Atleti thrashing of Barça
items.summary: Diego Simeone said Julián Álvarez's goal and assist in Atlético Madrid's 4-0 Copa del Rey thrashing of Barcelona will help the forward reset after ending an 11-game scoring drought.

items.title: Ange: Frank sacking 'fair,' but Spurs lack vision
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has labelled Thomas Frank's sacking a "fair departure" but sympathised with the Dane for having to work in an "environment of uncertainty."

items.title: Will Arsenal's end-of-season form haunt them again?
items.summary: Arsenal are four points clear at the top of the Premier League with 12 games to play - but will recent history repeat itself?

items.title: FA Cup fourth round: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Burnley have the chance of a Cup run, Leicester fear an unwelcome repeat and Brighton fans get a raw deal Chelsea have kept two clean sheets in 10 games since appointing Liam Rosenior as head coach last month. Repeated doziness at the back has cost them. They have held commanding advantages against Charlton, Crystal Palace, Wolves and Leeds, only to give away silly goals. It is a bad habit and proved costly when a 2-0 lead was squandered during Tuesday’s draw with Leeds. Rosenior was livid afterwards, and is waiting for a consistent performance. Chelsea travel to Hull , Rosenior’s former club, on Friday night. They will surely advance against Championship opponents, but how they do it will matter. It is time for them to get serious. Jacob Steinberg Hull City v Chelsea, Friday 7.45pm (all times GMT) Burton Albion v West Ham, Saturday 12.15pm Burnley v Mansfield, Saturday 3pm Southampton v Leicester, Saturday 3pm Continue reading...

items.title: Atlético Madrid put one foot in Copa del Rey final after first-half blitz stuns Barcelona
items.summary: Semi-final first leg: Atlético Madrid 4-0 Barcelona E García 6og, Griezmann 14, Lookman 33, Alvarez 45+2 You must always have faith, Diego Simeone had insisted and so it was. A biblical storm blew through the Metropolitano, leaving Barcelona in pieces and Atlético Madrid closer to a first Copa del Rey final in 13 years. “I’m not a wizard but I did believe that the team could play like this,” Simeone said at the end of a wild night, yet even he could not have imagined anything quite like this, 45 extraordinary minutes giving his team a 4-0 lead to take to the Camp Nou in three weeks’ time. “This will remain in the memory however the tie ends,” Simeone said, careful to note that this is not over yet. Hansi Flick, meanwhile, vowed that his Barcelona team will fight, claimed they had been handed a “great lesson” that might yet be helpful, and outlined a plan for the second leg: 2-0 in each half. But an an own goal from Eric García and three more before half-time here from Ademola Lookman, Antoine Griezmann and Julián Alvarez, did the kind of damage that will be mightily difficult to fix. Barcelona could not begin that task here, a Pau Cubarsí effort ruled out after a seven-minute VAR check the only “goal” of the second half. Indeed, another VAR check made their second leg task even harder when Eric García was sent off in the final minutes. Continue reading...
